# sdlc-mcp

A foundational SDLC repository that serves as the persistent memory layer and AI development toolkit across all agentic tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Roo/Cline, Windsurf, and others).

## Purpose

This repo gives AI assistants:
- Structured knowledge of the full software development lifecycle
- Active slash commands for creating SDLC deliverables
- A live state layer so any session in any tool can pick up where the last one left off

## How to fetch files from this repo

All files listed in this document are accessible via the `fetch_generic_url_content` tool using this URL pattern:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DanaKowalski/agentic-sdlc-mcp/main/<file-path>

Example: to fetch `sdlc/overview.md`, call `fetch_generic_url_content` with:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DanaKowalski/agentic-sdlc-mcp/main/sdlc/overview.md

Fetch files as you need them. Do not try to load all files at once — fetch what is relevant to the current task.

## How to orient at session start

### Fetch from sdlc-gitmcp (remote framework — read only)
1. Fetch `sdlc/memory/quick-ref.md`
2. Fetch `AGENTS.md`
3. Fetch `sdlc/overview.md`

### Read or create locally (your project directory)
4. Read `docs/memory/project-state.md` in your local project
   If it does not exist, this is a new project — create it locally before proceeding

Never create or write files to the sdlc-gitmcp server. It is read-only.
All project state, agent outputs, and docs live in your local project directory.

Then tell the user what you found and ask where they want to pick up.

Do not skip step 2. A session that starts without reading project state will duplicate work or contradict previous decisions.

## Available tools

- **context7** (stdio MCP): fetches current library/framework docs on demand
- **sdlc-gitmcp** (this server, SSE): live read access to this repo's files and state
- **sdlc** (stdio MCP, local): exposes slash commands

## Available commands

These commands are available when running the sdlc MCP server locally.
If you are connected via sdlc-gitmcp (remote), these commands are not available —
use the templates directly instead.

| Command | Template to use instead |
|---------|------------------------|
| `/generate_prd` | Fetch and follow `sdlc/planning/PRD-template.md` |
| `/create_technical_design` | Fetch and follow `sdlc/design/technical-design-template.md` |
| `/create_adr` | Fetch and follow `sdlc/design/architecture-decision-record-template.md` |
| `/plan_sprint` | No template yet — describe sprint goals in `docs/planning/` |
| `/gen_test_plan` | Fetch and follow `sdlc/testing/README.md` |
| `/release_notes` | Draft from git log manually |

## Subagent system

This repo uses a prescriptive subagent architecture. The main session agent (orchestrator) must spawn subagents in these situations:

- Codebase not read this session and task needs code knowledge → spawn research agent
- Task touches more than 3 unread files → spawn research agent
- Task has 2+ independent work units → spawn parallel implementation agents
- Task is destructive or high-risk → spawn isolated implementation agent
- Implementation complete → spawn review agent

Every subagent writes output to `docs/agents/<date>-<role>-<slug>.md`. Commit after each.

Full rules: fetch `sdlc/agents/orchestrator.md`

## Key files — fetch as needed

### Session and memory
- `sdlc/memory/quick-ref.md` — session open/close sequence, all commands, agent triggers (fetch at every session start)
- `docs/memory/project-state.md` — live project state (fetch at every session start)
- `sdlc/agents/memory-protocol.md` — full memory rules

### Agent system
- `AGENTS.md` — core agent rules and conventions
- `sdlc/agents/orchestrator.md` — orchestrator and subagent trigger rules
- `sdlc/agents/research-agent.md` — research agent prompt template and rules
- `sdlc/agents/design-agent.md` — design agent prompt template and rules
- `sdlc/agents/implementation-agent.md` — implementation agent prompt template and rules
- `sdlc/agents/review-agent.md` — review agent prompt template and rules

### Planning phase
- `sdlc/overview.md` — all SDLC phase definitions and rules
- `sdlc/planning/README.md` — planning phase workflow
- `sdlc/planning/PRD-template.md` — PRD template
- `sdlc/planning/planning-checklist.md` — planning phase checklist
- `sdlc/planning/user-story-guidelines.md` — user story and acceptance criteria guidelines

### Design phase
- `sdlc/design/README.md` — design phase workflow
- `sdlc/design/technical-design-template.md` — technical design template
- `sdlc/design/architecture-decision-record-template.md` — ADR template
- `sdlc/design/design-checklist.md` — design phase checklist

### Implementation phase
- `sdlc/implementation/README.md` — implementation phase workflow and gate conditions
- `sdlc/implementation/coding-standards.md` — coding conventions
- `sdlc/implementation/git-workflow.md` — branch and commit rules
- `sdlc/implementation/task-breakdown-template.md` — translate technical design into bounded tasks
- `sdlc/implementation/implementation-checklist.md` — implementation phase checklist
- `sdlc/implementation/testing-strategy.md` — test types, structure, and coverage expectations
- `sdlc/implementation/environment-setup.md` — environment setup steps

### Testing phase
- `sdlc/testing/README.md` — testing phase overview and test type index

## Session closing protocol

Before ending a session, update project state and commit:
git add docs/
git commit -m "docs([phase]): [brief description]"
git push

This ensures the next session — in any tool — can read current state via GitMCP.
